name: Broken Authentication
what_it_means: Identity verification is weak or incorrect: APIs accept missing, expired, or tampered tokens; sessions or keys can be replayed or swapped.
animated_example: Security gate where guards wave everyone through without checking IDs.
quick_checks: All protected endpoints require valid, unexpired tokens (OAuth2/OIDC/JWT)
Token signature/claims validated (iss, aud, exp, kid); no alg=none
Short-lived access tokens; rotating refresh tokens; revocation honored
MFA for sensitive actions; device binding where appropriate
TLS enforced; no credential exposure in URLs; no basic auth over HTTP
Default/weak credentials disallowed; keys rotated periodically
how_to_test: Call with no token, expired token, tampered signature
Replay token after logout/rotation; swap user_id claim
Remove required auth fields and observe behavior
Attempt brute-force login with rate-limit detection
expected_outcomes: 401 for missing/invalid token; 403 for valid but unauthorized
No successful calls with tampered or expired tokens; events logged
minimal_example: GET /profile without Authorization → 401; with expired JWT → 401; with valid token lacking scope → 403
